entry_facets.label: Rule
entry_facets.summary: General Xianxia rule reference entries that do not need a narrower facet.

entry_facets.label: Attribute
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for the six Attributes used by Xianxia checks and character creation.

entry_facets.label: Effort
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Basic, Weapon, Guns/Explosive, Magic, and Ultimate Effort values.

entry_facets.label: Energy
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Jing, Qi, and Shen maximums, current pools, and spend hooks.

entry_facets.label: Yin/Yang
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Yin and Yang pools and their GM-clarified action support.

entry_facets.label: Dao
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Dao gain, cap, persistence, and spend reminders.

entry_facets.label: Realm
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Mortal, Immortal, and Divine realm state and action counts.

entry_facets.label: Honor Rank
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Honor ranks and social interaction directionality.

entry_facets.label: Skill Rule
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for trained freeform skills and their non-combat boundaries.

entry_facets.label: Equipment
entry_facets.summary: Entries for weapons, tools, and other Xianxia equipment that should stay outside DND-5E item assumptions.

entry_facets.label: Armor
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for armor as Defense-bearing gear while detailed armor quality tiers remain deferred.

entry_facets.label: Martial Art
entry_facets.summary: Parent entries for shared Xianxia Martial Arts, including seeded and GM-created arts.

entry_facets.label: Martial Art Rank
entry_facets.summary: Structured rank entries or embedded rank records for Initiate, Novice, Apprentice, Master, and Legendary progress.

entry_facets.label: Technique
entry_facets.summary: Martial Art ability entries or rank-granted ability records tagged as Techniques.

entry_facets.label: Maneuver
entry_facets.summary: Martial Art ability entries or rank-granted ability records tagged as Maneuvers.

entry_facets.label: Stance
entry_facets.summary: Entries or ability records for one-active-Stance rules and Stance-granting Martial Art abilities.

entry_facets.label: Aura
entry_facets.summary: Entries or ability records for one-active-Aura rules and Aura-granting Martial Art abilities.

entry_facets.label: Generic Technique
entry_facets.summary: Standalone purchasable Generic Techniques and Maneuvers learned with Insight after character creation.

entry_facets.label: Condition
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for condition concepts without automated condition timing, targeting, or combat-state effects in Milestone 1.

entry_facets.label: Status
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for status-effect concepts while the Xianxia status taxonomy and status functionality remain deferred.

entry_facets.label: Range Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Touch, Close, Near, Far, Space, and feet-based area dimensions.

entry_facets.label: Timing Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Turn, Round, Day, initiative order, and once-per-combat timing.

entry_facets.label: Critical Hit Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Critical Hit triggers and Ultimate Effort damage reminders.

entry_facets.label: Sneak Attack Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Sneak Attack as a special Critical Hit enabled by explicit circumstances.

entry_facets.label: Dying Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Dying state rules without implementing Dying Rounds tracking in Milestone 1.

entry_facets.label: Minion Tag
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Minion-tagged NPC concepts without encounter seeding or combat automation.

entry_facets.label: Companion Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for puppet, phantom, animal, and similar companion derivation rules without richer companion automation.

entry_facets.label: GM Approval Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for explicit GM approval gates such as Karmic Constraints, Ascendant Arts, and Dao Immolating use.

martial_art_ranks.advancement_note: Progressing in a Martial Art requires 1 Insight and the requisites for the target rank; starting character packages can grant ranks separately.
Progressing in a Martial Art requires 1 Insight and the requisites for the target rank; starting character packages can grant ranks separately.

martial_art_ranks.teacher_breakthrough_note: Requires learning under a Master. Teacher requirements are stored as notes for now rather than enforced advancement blockers.
martial_art_ranks.advancement_note: Progressing in a Martial Art requires 1 Insight and the requisites for the target rank; starting character packages can grant ranks separately.

martial_art_ranks.teacher_breakthrough_note: Requires learning under a Legendary Master. Teacher requirements are stored as notes for now rather than enforced advancement blockers.
martial_art_ranks.advancement_note: Progressing in a Martial Art requires 1 Insight and the requisites for the target rank; starting character packages can grant ranks separately.
Progressing in a Martial Art requires 1 Insight and the requisites for the target rank; starting character packages can grant ranks separately.
martial_art_ranks.legendary_prerequisite_note: Requires all previous ranks in the Martial Art plus a quest or mythic-level master; primarily narrative completion rather than a purely mechanical purchase.

entries.title: Checks and Difficulty
entries.summary: Xianxia checks roll 1d20 plus an Attribute, Realm modifier, and situational modifiers against a final DC.

sections.title: Check Formula
paragraphs: A normal check is 1d20 + Attribute + Realm modifier + situational modifiers. Add +1d6 for each point of appropriate Energy, Yin, or Yang spent. A result that equals or exceeds the final DC succeeds.

entries.title: EASY, Normal, and HARD
entries.summary: EASY, Normal, and HARD are final DC states, not unbounded stacking bonuses.

sections.title: DC States
bullets: EASY lowers the final DC by 3.
Normal leaves the final DC unchanged.
HARD raises the final DC by 3.
EASY and HARD influences shift the final state against each other instead of stacking into larger bonuses or penalties.

entries.title: Attributes
entries.summary: Xianxia uses the six standard Attributes for checks and character creation budgets.

sections.title: Attribute Set
bullets: The six Attributes are Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma.
Starting characters spend 6 creation points across Attributes.
No Attribute can exceed 3 at character creation unless the GM explicitly overrides the budget.

entries.title: Efforts and Damage
entries.summary: Efforts are separate from Attributes and add their score to the matching effort die for damage.

sections.title: Effort Families
bullets: Basic Effort uses 1d4 + Basic.
Weapon Effort uses 1d6 + Weapon.
Guns/Explosive Effort uses 1d8 + Guns/Explosive.
Magic Effort uses 1d10 + Magic.
Ultimate Effort uses 1d12 + Ultimate.

sections.title: Creation Budget
bullets: Starting characters spend 5 creation points across Efforts.
No Effort can exceed 3 at character creation unless the GM explicitly overrides the budget.

entries.title: Energy: Jing, Qi, and Shen
entries.summary: Jing, Qi, and Shen are spendable Energies with separate maximums and action themes.

sections.title: Energy Uses
bullets: Jing supports physical actions.
Qi supports mystical or skill-based actions.
Shen supports spiritual or magical actions.
A character may spend an appropriate Energy point for +1d6 on a check.
Starting characters spend 3 creation points across Jing, Qi, and Shen.

entries.title: Yin and Yang
entries.summary: Yin and Yang are separate spendable pools that start at 1 each.

sections.title: GM-Ruled Action Support
bullets: Yin supports offensive or physical actions.
Yang supports defensive or magical actions.
A character may spend an appropriate Yin or Yang point for +1d6 on a check.
Yin and Yang recover with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Dao
entries.summary: Dao is a capped narrative and combat resource that persists until spent.

sections.title: Dao Rules
bullets: Dao is capped at 3.
Dao starts at 0 unless the GM explicitly grants accumulated Dao.
Dao may be gained through Energy spend, roleplay, or certain Martial Arts.
Dao does not reset at session start or after rest.
Dao is held until spent on difficulty reduction or later Dao actions.

entries.title: Hit Points
entries.summary: Hit Points are the character's injury track and start with a maximum of 10.

sections.title: HP Baseline
bullets: Starting HP maximum is 10.
Current HP is clamped to the current HP maximum.
Dropping to 0 HP puts the character in the Dying state.
HP recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Stance
entries.summary: Stance is a second durability track that starts with a maximum of 10.

sections.title: Stance Baseline
bullets: Starting Stance maximum is 10.
Current Stance is clamped to the current Stance maximum.
When current Stance reaches 0, the character's Stance breaks.
Stance recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Defense
entries.summary: Defense is derived from base 10, armor bonus, and Constitution.

sections.title: Formula
paragraphs: Defense equals 10 + armor bonus + Constitution. Milestone 1 uses a manual armor bonus until detailed armor quality and stat tiers are defined.

entries.title: Insight
entries.summary: Insight is the advancement currency for Martial Arts, cultivation, training, meditation, conditioning, and Generic Techniques.

sections.title: Insight Baseline
bullets: Insight starts at 0.
Generic Techniques cannot be learned at character creation because they require spending Insight unless the GM grants an exception.
Martial Art teacher requirements are notes for now rather than enforced advancement blockers.

entries.title: Realm and Actions
entries.summary: Realm defines major character tier, action count, and ascension rebuild rules.

sections.title: Realm Actions
bullets: Mortal characters have 2 total Actions.
Immortal characters have 3 total Actions.
Divine characters have 4 total Actions.

sections.title: Ascension Reference
bullets: Immortal ascension requires 1 year of seclusion, rebuilds Attributes and Efforts with 15 points, and has a starting max of 6 in any one Stat.
Divine ascension requires 100 years of seclusion, rebuilds Attributes and Efforts with 25 points, and has a starting max of 12 in any one Stat.
Realm ascension resets only Attributes and Efforts; Energies, Yin/Yang, HP, Stance, Insight, Martial Arts, Generic Techniques, and other character data remain unchanged.

entries.title: Honor
entries.summary: Honor is a directional social modifier tied to orthodox, demonic, and criminal contexts.

sections.title: Honor Interactions
bullets: Honorable is neutral and is the default starting Honor.
Venerable and Majestic grant +3 and +5 with orthodox sects and individuals.
Disgraced and Demonic grant +3 and +5 with demonic or criminal backgrounds.
When dealing with the opposite Honor alignment, the same value applies as a penalty.

entries.title: Reputation
entries.summary: Reputation starts at Unknown and is a narrative roleplay convenience rather than a hard mechanic.

sections.title: Reputation Baseline
paragraphs: Reputation should record how a character is known in the world, but Milestone 1 does not treat it as an automated check modifier.

entries.title: Skills
entries.summary: Skills are freeform trained non-combat specialties that can make relevant checks EASY.

sections.title: Skill Guardrails
bullets: Starting characters choose exactly 3 trained skills.
A trained skill can make a relevant check EASY.
Skills cannot be used in active battle to affect Attacks or Damage.
Skills can affect surroundings or pre-battle preparation when the GM agrees.

entries.title: Basic Actions
entries.summary: Basic Actions are core Xianxia action references; individual automation remains deferred.

sections.title: Known Basic Actions
paragraphs: The core Basic Action reference covers Recoup, Recollect, Duel, Taunt, Wide Dispatch, Flowing Dao, Throat Jab, Knuckle Strike, Defend, All-Out Offense, and Parry.

entries.title: Cultivation Time
entries.summary: Cultivation time records advancement downtime and GM-reviewed growth rather than a combat automation loop.

sections.title: Downtime Reference
bullets: Gathering Insight is a downtime gain path.
Cultivation, Meditation, Conditioning, Training, Martial Art rank advancement, and Generic Technique learning spend Insight later in advancement workflows.
Realm ascension seclusion uses the Realm timing rules: 1 year for Immortal and 100 years for Divine.
Milestone 1 does not add calendar automation for cultivation time.

entries.title: One-Day Rest
entries.summary: One day of rest recovers the normal Xianxia pools except Dao unless another effect blocks recovery.

sections.title: Recovery
bullets: Outside battle, HP recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Outside battle, Stance recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Energy, Yin, and Yang recover with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Dao does not reset after rest and remains held until spent.

entries.title: Ranges and Distance
entries.summary: Xianxia range labels map to feet, adjacency, and 5-foot spaces.

sections.title: Range Labels
bullets: Touch requires physical contact through a Melee Attack.
Close means within 5 feet, adjacent, or dueling.
Near means up to 30 feet.
Far means up to 60 feet.
One Space is a 5-foot square.
Area dimensions such as 30x30 are measured in feet.

entries.title: Timing and Initiative
entries.summary: Timing terms define turns, rounds, days, initiative order, and once-per-combat limits.

sections.title: Timing Terms
bullets: A Turn is one character's time for Actions in combat.
A Round is the full pass through every participant's Turn in initiative order.
Initiative is set at the beginning of combat with a Dex Check, runs highest to lowest, and is not rerolled each Round.
A Day is 24 hours.
Once-per-combat means once per combat encounter.

entries.title: Stance Activation Rules
entries.summary: A character can have only one Stance active at a time.

sections.title: Active Stance
bullets: Entering a Stance costs an Action plus any Stance-specific costs.
A Stance ends when the character's Stance breaks.
A Stance ends when the character switches Stances.
A Stance ends when the character can no longer maintain the Stance's requirements.

entries.title: Aura Activation Rules
entries.summary: A character can have only one Aura active at a time.

sections.title: Active Aura
paragraphs: Auras are assumed to remain active once activated unless the Aura says otherwise or the GM overrules it.

entries.title: Dying and Unconsciousness
entries.summary: Dropping to 0 HP puts a character in the Dying state; Dying Rounds tracking remains deferred automation.

sections.title: Dying Reference
bullets: When a character drops to 0 HP, the player rolls 1d6; the result is the number of Dying Rounds available.
During those Dying Rounds, the character must roll a natural 20 on a raw d20, be healed by an ally, receive item healing, or have the battle end to stop dying.
A dying character is unconscious, cannot take Actions, cannot interact or speak, and is unaware of their surroundings.
Milestone 1 stores this as reference text and does not implement Dying Rounds tracking.

entries.title: Critical Hits
entries.summary: Critical Hits automatically hit and add Ultimate Effort damage.

sections.title: Critical Hit Reference
paragraphs: Critical Hits occur on a natural 20, or on a lower natural roll when modified by a Martial Art or Technique. Critical Hits automatically hit and deal additional +Ultimate Effort damage.

entries.title: Sneak Attacks
entries.summary: Sneak Attack is a special kind of Critical Hit enabled by explicit circumstances.

sections.title: Sneak Attack Reference
paragraphs: Sneak Attack only occurs under specific circumstances, such as a Martial Art or Technique explicitly enabling it or the target being completely off guard.

entries.title: Minions
entries.summary: Minion is a tag for generic fodder enemies and martial artists.

sections.title: Minion Reference
paragraphs: Minions are NPCs whose Realm and HP/Stance are lower than the player characters, usually fragile enough to be removed in a single blow, such as an NPC with only 5 HP and 5 Stance.

entries.title: Companion Derivation
entries.summary: Puppets, phantoms, animals, and similar companions derive from the user's Stats unless their creating ability says otherwise.

sections.title: Companion Reference
paragraphs: Companions usually use half the user's Stats plus any listed modifications from the ability that creates or commands them. Richer companion automation is deferred.

entries.title: GM Approval Gates
entries.summary: Karmic Constraints, Ascendant Arts, and Dao Immolating Techniques require explicit GM approval.

sections.title: Approval Requirements
bullets: Karmic Constraints require explicit GM approval in addition to their Insight cost.
Ascendant Arts require explicit GM approval in addition to their Insight cost.
Dao Immolating Techniques may be prepared ahead of time or invented at use time; preparation is not required, but it can make GM approval easier.
Dao Immolating Techniques require explicit GM approval at the time of use and are more narrative than mechanical.

entries.title: Bing Ti Legendary Errata
entries.summary: Bing Ti's Legendary rank uses Jing wherever the reviewed draft says Ji.

sections.title: Correction
bullets: In Bing Ti's Legendary rank, read Ji as Jing.
This records the GM clarification only; the Martial Art catalog entry remains deferred to the Martial Arts seed slice.
